journal_red_flags: Lack of standard video coding benchmarks (e.g., VTM, HM, JEM) or standard video datasets
Purely empirical deep learning papers without circuit, system, or video-specific technical insights
Omitting computational complexity, bitrate-performance trade-off analysis (e.g., BD-rate), or hardware implementation details
Presenting related work as an unstructured chronological list without categorical subheadings or highlighting specific limitations
Omitting comprehensive ablation studies to justify design choices for newly proposed architectural modules
Relying on purely theoretical derivations or heavy mathematical proofs without sufficient empirical validation on standard benchmarks
Using informal, non-mathematical problem formulations or first-person singular narrative in technical and methodology sections
Evaluating only on basic tasks (e.g., image classification) without dense prediction or video-specific tasks when claiming a general-purpose backbone
